x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces MOME's flagship open data (Film Permits, the ScreenActivity held locations inside them, MARCH nightlife inspections, and MOME programs) and adds apply_for_film_permit — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the login-walled 'MOME E-Apply' ASP.NET portal.

server.name: io.nyc.mome
server.title: NYC Mayor's Office of Media & Entertainment (MOME)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Mayor's Office of Media & Entertainment — the famous Film Permits open dataset (every location shoot the Film Office greenlights, updated daily), the held street locations inside each permit, MARCH nightlife inspections, and MOME's programs (Made in NY, Office of Nightlife, Public Access Media) — plus the net-new ability to apply for and track a film/photography permit that today lives only in the login-walled MOME E-Apply portal.
server.instructions: Use find_film_permits / get_film_permit to search issued permits by borough, production category, or date, and get_film_permit_locations (or find_screen_activity) to see exactly which streets a shoot holds. find_march_inspections covers Office of Nightlife MARCH enforcement. find_programs enumerates MOME's non-permit surface (Made in NY, workforce, Public Access Media). apply_for_film_permit (net-new) submits a film-permit application; it requires an authenticated applicant and mutates state — confirm the production, category, dates, and locations with the user before submitting, and note that insurance must be on file before a permit is issued (or set letterInLieu for low-impact exterior shoots).

tools.name: find_film_permits
tools.title: Find Film Permits
tools.description: Search issued NYC film/photography permits by borough, production category, event type, sub-category, date range, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_film_permit
tools.title: Get Film Permit
tools.description: Fetch a single issued film permit (type, production category, dates, held streets, geography) by EventID.

tools.name: get_film_permit_locations
tools.title: Get Film Permit Locations
tools.description: List the held screen-activity street segments within a film permit (the permit's ParkingHeld exploded).

tools.name: find_screen_activity
tools.title: Find Screen Activity
tools.description: Search held film locations across all permits — where is filming happening, by street, borough, or date.

tools.name: find_programs
tools.title: Find Programs
tools.description: List MOME programs and designated resources — Made in NY, Office of Nightlife, workforce/training, press credentials, and Public Access Media providers.

tools.name: find_march_inspections
tools.title: Find MARCH Inspections
tools.description: Search Office of Nightlife MARCH multi-agency inspections by borough, council district, or date.

tools.name: list_my_permit_applications
tools.title: List My Permit Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own film-permit applications and their status — today visible only inside the MOME E-Apply portal.

tools.name: apply_for_film_permit
tools.title: Apply for a Film Permit
tools.description: Submit a NYC film/photography permit application. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the login-walled 'MOME E-Apply' ASP.NET portal or a Letter-in-Lieu email. Requires an authenticated applicant. Mutates state — confirm production, category, dates, and held locations first; insurance must be on file before a permit is issued.
